## Summary

Raise Storygate eligibility threshold from 80 to ≥90, neutralize prescriptive revision labels (Must/Should/Could → Recommended/Optional/Consider) across all author-facing views, and fix download formatting across TXT/DOCX/PDF exports — governance data stripped, spacing/indentation/branding standardized, proper breathing room between sections.

## Scope

**Touched:**
- Workbench UI components (9 files): label rendering, severity display
- Download route (`app/api/reports/[jobId]/download/route.ts`): TXT/DOCX/PDF export formatting, governance stripping, branding
- Dashboard screening (`lib/dashboard/getDashboardEvaluations.ts`): threshold raised to 9.0
- Storygate screening (`screenStorygateSubmission/entry.ts`): threshold raised to 9.0

**NOT touched:** evaluation pipeline, API workers, scoring logic, prompt templates, database schema.

## Visual Evidence

| View | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Severity labels | MUST / SHOULD / COULD / Kept | RECOMMENDED / OPTIONAL / CONSIDER / Author Kept Original |
| Storygate threshold | ≥80 (8.0) | ≥90 (9.0) |
| Verdict tiers | 80+ Market Ready | <60 Not Ready, 60-89 Revise, ≥90 Market Ready |
| TXT export | Governance included, 4-space indent, tight spacing | Governance stripped, 2-space indent, breathing room |
| DOCX export | Governance sections rendered, tight spacing | Governance stripped, increased spacing |
| PDF footer | RevisionGrade(TM), Page N | RevisionGrade™, Page N of M |
| PDF bullets | Dash bullets, tight sections | Bullet char (•), increased spacing |

## Accessibility

No new interactive surfaces added. Label text changes maintain existing contrast ratios and ARIA structure.

## Browser Targets

- Chrome latest
- Safari latest
- Firefox latest

## Unauthorized Input Sources

None — all changes are to rendering/display logic. No new input sources introduced.

## Internal Process Leakage

Internal governance data (WAVE, Gate 15, Golden Spine, Dialogue Canon, Revision Canon) explicitly stripped from all customer-facing TXT and DOCX downloads. These sections are now only accessible via admin support view. No internal IDs, worker state, or stack traces exposed.

## Input → Action → Output

- User requests TXT/DOCX/PDF download → route reads canonical `longform_document_v1` artifact → renders with updated labels, no governance data, proper formatting → returns file
- Workbench displays revision items → reads same artifact → renders with Recommended/Optional/Consider labels
- Dashboard lists evaluations → screening query uses 9.0 threshold → manuscripts scoring below 90 show "Revise" verdict

Failure path: if artifact missing, existing fallback logic returns error response (unchanged).

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

Verdict labels displayed to users: "Not Ready" (below 60), "Revise" (60-89), "Market Ready" (90+). These are public-safe status indicators with no internal telemetry leakage.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

None — no new runtime calls, workers, API routes, or pipeline paths added. All changes are to existing rendering/display logic.

## Latency Impact

No measurable latency impact — changes are to string formatting, spacing values, and conditional rendering. Governance data removal reduces TXT/DOCX output size slightly.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Existing evaluations with scores 80-89 will now show "Revise" instead of qualifying for Storygate — intended behavior change
- Governance data removal is irreversible in exports (admin support view still has access)
- PDF bullet character uses Unicode (•) which all modern PDF viewers support

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — this PR does not modify lib/evaluation/**, app/api/workers/**, prompts, or any pipeline contract.
